description: In C, run the package-local validation gate (make / Makefile targets (and the exact CC, CFLAGS, and -I/-L flags)) and summarize the diagnostics.
body: In C, this capability runs the owning package's gate and reports the outcome so the next action is obvious. Use when you need an authoritative pass/fail for a C package before or after a change. Do not run a root or generic command that bypasses the package's real config, and avoid reporting success without the exit code. Actions: take the package path, run cc -Wall -Wextra -Werror with the same standard (-std=c11) the build uses then clang-tidy, cppcheck, and -fanalyzer warnings and the unit binary plus valgrind on the changed translation unit, and record the command, exit code, the first compiler error with file:line and the caret-marked token, and the recommended next step. Validation signals include the captured command log, the exit code, and the first diagnostics with file and line. Risk boundary: surface, do not auto-fix; escalate when the gate requires network access, credentials, or a toolchain change.
